flashcards.back: Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle where the last inserted element is removed first.

flashcards.front: Name two real-world applications of queues.
flashcards.back: 1) Printer job scheduling 2) CPU task scheduling
Double-Ended Queue allowing insertions/deletions at both ends.

flashcards.front: What operation adds an element to a stack?
flashcards.back: Push operation adds elements to the top of the stack.
Attempting to pop an element from an empty stack.

flashcards.front: Which data structure is used in BFS algorithm?
flashcards.back: Queue ensures First-In-First-Out processing of nodes.

flashcards.front: What's the time complexity of deque operations in a linked implementation?
flashcards.back: O(1) for add/remove at both ends using doubly linked list.

flashcards.front: How is circular queue better than linear queue?
flashcards.back: Reuses empty spaces created after dequeues, preventing false overflow.

flashcards.front: What's the main limitation of array-based queue implementation?
flashcards.back: Fixed size leading to overflow even with available space in circular implementation.

flashcards.front: Which structure would you use for undo functionality?
flashcards.back: Stack - each undo pops the last state from history.

flashcards.front: What's the postfix notation of A+B*C?
How to implement queue using two stacks?
flashcards.back: Use one stack for enqueue, another for dequeue. Transfer elements when dequeue stack is empty.
